title: `podman generate kube` - YAML when without shared network
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am not sure if this is a bug, not added feature intentionally or feature not yet implemented.
`podman generate kube` for pod without shared network produces `YAML` file which doesn't reproduce pod state when "played".

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pod create --name nginxs-pod --replace=true --share pid,ipc,uts`

2. `podman run --detach -t --pod nginxs-pod --name nginx1 --replace=true -p 30080:80/TCP --rm=true docker.io/library/nginx:latest`

3. `podman run --detach -t --pod nginxs-pod --name nginx2 --replace=true -p 30081:80/TCP --rm=true docker.io/library/nginx:latest`

4. `podman generate kube nginxs-pod > nginxs-pod.yaml`

5.  `podman pod rm -f nginxs-pod`

6.  `podman play kube nginxs-pod.yaml`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5                                  6 seconds ago  Up 3 seconds ago              6582b85b15e6-infra
docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  4 seconds ago  Up 3 seconds ago              nginxs-pod-nginx2
```
`nginx1` is not available (because of `Address already in use`).

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                  NAMES
k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5                                  37 seconds ago  Up 15 seconds ago                         ada9c5c3d567-infra
docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  15 seconds ago  Up 15 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:30080->80/tcp  nginx1
docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  3 seconds ago   Up 2 seconds ago   0.0.0.0:30081->80/tcp  nginx2
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.3.0-rc1
API Version:  3.3.0-rc1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug`

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:3.2.3-3 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.2.3-3 arm64

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body: Does the k8s yaml has support for specifying which namespaces should be shared? I only found this https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/workload-resources/pod-v1/#hosts-namespaces

This one may be outside what Kube YAML can do. I expect we're going to run into this more and more as we start to push the boundaries with `play kube` - probably worth a discussion with the team about approach on issues like this. @baude @rhatdan WDYT?

Yes I think we need to say that certain features that are not implemented in kubernetes, will not translate back into generate kube, because they can not.
podman pod create `pods` are not completely identical to Kubernetes Pods.  Similarly containers can not be created identically.

Thanks for the answers!

This is what I expected. Maybe kind of warning for not translatable features during `podman generate kube` might be added to inform about this?

What command should be used instead to dump set of settings for further usage? `podman-compose`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is not really an issue, and I hesitate to have warning about missing features, because we will miss many. Lets continue the conversation as a discussion.
